summary: Strong act-level escalation, but the window overstates Damian's hidden recognition across chapters 13-15 and loses its sharpest boundary pressure by skipping the immediate Loid-Anya reckoning after the summit.

findings.evidence: Chapter 13 already makes Damian's recognition explicit in the photo scene and states that he will keep working with Anya without telling her yet (snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7185, snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7217). Chapter 14 then spends fresh beats on him suppressing 'Eden' and swapping in safer thoughts when he enters the Forger apartment (snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7748, snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7824).
findings.problem: Read back-to-back, chapter 14 re-explains a plot fact the reader already has in full: Damian knows exactly who Anya is and is managing what she can catch. Because chapter 13 has already converted that from suspicion into certainty, the new concealment beats in chapter 14 play less like fresh tension and more like repeated dramatic irony.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep one reminder beat when Damian enters the apartment, then spend the rest of his page time on a new consequence of that knowledge: a sharper tactical choice, a changed emotional reaction to the Forger household, or a concrete risk he is now actively managing.
findings.acceptance_test: After reading chapters 13 and 14 consecutively, the reader gets no more than one reminder that Damian knows Anya's identity before the scene delivers a materially new consequence of that knowledge.
findings.fix_owner_reason: Later chapter 14 owns this because chapter 13 has already delivered the recognition cleanly; chapter 14 repeats it through additional telepathic-dodge signaling.

findings.evidence: Chapter 15 ends with Loid keeping Anya with him, refusing any partial explanation, and demanding the full account once they are home (snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:8809, snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:8813). Chapter 16 then opens by rerouting to Damian because 'Damian had to come first,' without showing how the promised Loid confrontation was delayed or handled (snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:8828, snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:8829).
findings.problem: The chapter boundary primes an immediate father-handler reckoning as the next live scene, then steps around it. Because chapter 15 ends on Loid's coldest possible demand, skipping the bridge into chapter 16 makes the reader feel the book has dodged the freshest consequence in the cluster.
findings.rewrite_direction: Bridge the handoff explicitly. Either give a short on-page home beat with Loid before Anya goes to Damian, or establish on the first page of chapter 16 that Loid deliberately postponed the conversation overnight and why Anya is now able to leave before answering him.
findings.acceptance_test: When moving from chapter 15 to chapter 16, the reader can state in one sentence whether Loid's demanded explanation already happened, was consciously postponed, or is still imminent, without having to infer the missing bridge.
findings.fix_owner_reason: Later chapter 16 owns the break because chapter 15 correctly plants the obligation and chapter 16 is where the on-page handoff is omitted.

findings.evidence: Chapter 14 already locks in the summit geometry and assignments on the kitchen whiteboard: Yor to foyer junction and south perimeter, Loid to east corridor, Damian to West Gallery, and Anya to East Gallery press (snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:7964, snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:8020). Chapter 15 then opens by re-describing the hall layout and Anya's East Gallery placement before the action starts (snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:8025, snapshots/cycle_03/FINAL_NOVEL.md:8029).
findings.problem: On a consecutive read, the action chapter spends its opening energy rebuilding geography the previous chapter has just painstakingly taught the reader. That repeated re-orientation blunts the launch into the summit and makes chapter 15 feel slower off the line than the chapter break promises.
findings.rewrite_direction: Trust the reader's memory from chapter 14. Compress the venue reminder to one or two danger-bearing details, then move immediately into the operative signal Anya catches so the chapter opens on fresh threat instead of refreshed mapwork.
findings.acceptance_test: If the reader finishes chapter 14 and immediately starts chapter 15, the first page adds new threat information faster than it repeats summit layout, and no major position or route has to be re-learned from scratch.
findings.fix_owner_reason: Later chapter 15 owns this because chapter 14 has already done the orientation work; the redundant re-description happens when the action chapter starts.
